title: Crepidomanes intramarginale
taxonomy: Crepidomanes intramarginale is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. This species belongs to the genus Crepidomanes, which comprises about 400 species of filmy ferns. Crepidomanes intramarginale is native to tropical and subtropical regions, primarily in Asia and Australia. It is a type of epiphytic fern that grows on trees and rocks, and is often found in humid, shaded environments. The classification of Crepidomanes intramarginale is further supported by its unique morphological characteristics, such as its delicate fronds and small spore-producing structures.
uses: Crepidomanes intramarginale, also known as the "creeping maidenhair fern," has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. The plant's rhizomes and leaves are reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat fever, coughs, and respiratory issues. Additionally, it is believed to have antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for wound healing and preventing infections. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Crepidomanes intramarginale for medicinal purposes.
